title: Neobesseya robbinsiorum
intro: Neobesseya robbinsiorum, also known as Robbins' besseya, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Gentianaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Neobesseya robbinsiorum include alkaloids and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Robbins' besseya is used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and support immune function.
taxonomy: Neobesseya robbinsiorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The genus Neobesseya is a small group of plants closely related to the genus Gentiana. Neobesseya robbinsiorum is typically classified as a perennial herb with distinctive leaf and flower characteristics. Its classification as a species within the Gentianaceae family is well-supported by botanical research.
